titleStmt.title: SII 28.311: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 311. (A.R. No. 465 of 1913.)

p.hi: Achyutadēva-mahārāya Śaka 1453, Vikriti, Aṟpaśi 25, śu. 12, Monday, Puṇarpūśam Irregular
p.note: of. A.R.Ep., 1914, p. 68. L.D.Swamikannu Pillai has stated that 1530 A.D. 24 October was Aṟpaśi 25 and it was Monday. Even according to his own Indian Ephemeris, Vol. V, Aṟpaśi 25 was equivalent to october 25, which was a Tuesday in the year 1530 A.D. corresponded to Vikṛiti but the Śaka year was 1453 current.

summary.p: This inscription records that Chāma-nayiṉār-atiyāriyaḷ (adhikārigaḷ), the agent of Taṇa[Kishṇa]rāya-nāyakkar granted as mānyam, the village, Paṭṭilūr-kālauḷḷi-Tagaḍappāḍi-pāḷaiyam alias Vaḍivuḍaiya-maṅgaipuram to God Biṟamīśvaramuḍaiya-tambirāṉār.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . śrīmanmahāmaṇḍaleśvarametinimiśura kaṇḍa kaṭṭāri cāḷuva cāḷuva śrīvirapiṟatāpa pūṟuva tekṣaṇa paccima Uttara satucamutṟātipatiyāṉa Accutateva mahārāyaṉ pṛdhivi rājyam paṇṇi Aruḷā niṉṟa cakābdam 1000 4 100 5 10 3 mel cellā niṉṟa vikṛti varuṣam Aṟpaci mātam 2 10 5tiyati pūṟuva pakṣattu
tuvāteciyum puṇarpūcamum peṟṟa comavārattil taṇa kiṣṇarāya nāyakkaṉ kāriyattukku kaṭavarāṉa cāma nayiṉār Atiyāriyaḷ piṟamīcuramuṭaiya tampirāṉāṟku māṉiyamāka viṭṭa paṭṭilūr kāla Uḷḷi takaṭappāṭi pāḷaiyamāṉa vaṭivuṭaiya maṅkaipuram cantiṟātittavaraikkum yinta kiṟāmam piṟamīcuraṉukku naṭakka kaṭavatākavum yinta taṉmattai naṭattiṉavaṉ cāyūcciya patam peṟakkaṭavarākavum yinta kiṟāmattukku Akitam paṇṇiṉavaṉ keṅkai karaiyil kārām pacuvai koṉṟa
pāvattile pokavumOn top of the sixth piece there is a single line of writing in late characters running as follows malliya • ti • ma ••••• nākaṉ • sadāse vai ṭhere also two seperate pieces with a single line in late characters running as follows ī ve[l] piḷ[ḷai] īī • kakkum piṟi[vu] kaṅkaṉ [pa]ṭṭi narai malaṉ paṭṭiyum piṟivu tāṉattār campaḷattukkuppa
Digital edition of SII 28.311 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
